rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Objections And Assertions Of Privilege By Deft To Compel Further Responses For Falure To Provide Timely Responses To Special Interrogatrories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 7, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS GARY GROTE, JANICE GROTE AND EREN HUSSEIN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Objections And Assertions Of Privilege By Defendant, To Compel Further Responses For Failure To Provide Timely Responses To Special Interrogatories. Granted. Defendant shall provide objection-free responses and pay $1140 ($275 per hour for four hours plus $40 in costs) in sanctions by December 30, 2011. Defendant¿s position lacks substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA OCHOA et al VS. ERLINDA SAN JUAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion For Judgemnt Creditors For A Finding Of Contempt As To Defts For The Failure To Comply With Two Court Orders
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 7, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF BRENDA OCHOA, EVE SEGASTURE, JENNER OCHOA AND MICHAEL CIOFALO'S Notice Of Motion And motion For Judgment Creditors For A Finding Of Contempt As To Defendants For The Failure To Comply With Two Court Orders. Off calendar as to the defendants and denied as to defendants¿ counsel. Due to the bankruptcy filings by defendants and the ensuing automatic stay, the court is without authority to hear the motion as to defendants. Even assuming that the bankruptcy stay does not extend to the requests for sanctions and contempt finding against defendants¿ counsel, because the asserted basis for such sanctions and contempt is defendants¿ alleged misconduct and defendant are now unable to dispute such asserted misconduct, the court exercises its discretion to deny those requests. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOMENIC J CANNIZZARO et al VS. FRED P RICHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 7, 2011, Line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT DOMENIC CANNIZZARO DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days to answer. Although it appears that the claims alleged against Frank Cannizzaro may not proceed due the failure to comply with Probate Code 9370, this failure is not a ¿defect¿ challengeable by a demurrer and, while arguably a misjoinder, Domenic Cannizzaro has not shown that he is prejudiced by or his interests are impaired by the misjoinder. (Weil & Brown 7:78-77:83.2). The first, eight and eleventh causes of action are adequately pled. Any further information that Domenic Cannizzaro desires about those claims can be obtained through discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BERKELEY FARMS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MANJIT K. SHERGILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 7, 2011, Line 20. CROSS DEFENDANT BERKELEY FARMS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY, DEAN FOODS INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied as to summary judgment and summary adjudication of the fourth and sixth causes of action, and granted as to summary adjudication of the first, second, third and fifth causes of action. Cross-defendants satisfied their burden of production and CCI did not present admissible evidence creating a triable issue that Berkeley Farms made secret payments to or provided unearned discounts to any of CCI¿s competitors (first cause of action), cross-defendants could legally conspire with each other (second and third causes of action), or cross-defendants committed independently wrongful conduct to disrupt CCI¿s relationship with Santa Clara Dairy (fifth cause of action). There are triable issues regarding the application of Commercial Code 2201(2) (fourth cause of action) and whether CCI had contractual relationships with its customers (sixth cause of action). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Each side is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND BERRIOS VS. JOSE BERRIOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 7, 2011, Line 9. DEFENDANT JOSE BERRIOS DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. All of plaintiff¿s claims are fatally deficient for two reasons. First, all of the claims are time-barred. The gravamen of plaintiff¿s claims is that defendant failed to transfer title to the property to plaintiff. Yet paragragh 36 of the second amended complaint alleges that in 2002 defendant, after being asked to do so by plaintiff, refused to do so. The statute of limitations as to all of plaintiff¿s claims began to run no later than this refusal. Second, all of plaintiff¿s claims are based on the premise that the family company owned the property and it was the family company that agreed to transfer the property to plaintiff as compensation tor plaintiff¿s work for the company. This premise means that any claims that plaintiff has pertaining to ownership of the property should be directed against the family company, not defendant, and it appears that the family company ceased to exist many years ago. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GREG SCHOEPP, DBA BAY AREA COMPASSION HEALTH CARE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BY AND THROUGH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearign On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 7, 2011, Line 12. PETITIONER GREG SCHOEPP, DBA BAY AREA COMPASSION HEALTH CARE Motion For Hearign On Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Denied. Respondent did not act in excess of its jurisdiction and did not abuse its discretion. The appeals were timely filed. Respondent lawfully acted pursuant to its discretionary authority under the San Francisco Charter Section 4.106. (Lindell Company v. Board of Permit Appeals of CCSF (1943) 23 Cal.2d 303; Guinnane v. San Francisco City Planning Commission (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 732). The Board¿s decision was supported by findings and the findings were supported by substantial evidence. (See, e.g., AR 0186, 0217-0218, 0220-0225, 0228-0229, 0232). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
